name: zarchive_mount_index_archive
description: zArchive::MountIndexArchive allocates and resets a zIndexArchive, initializes it from a tail-index archive, optionally installs it as current, appends it to the mounted archive list, and cleans up on failure.
known_limits: VC5SP3 cl 11.00.7022 x86 /O2 /Ob1 /GX /Zp4 /FAcs COFF byte comparison for ?MountIndexArchive@zArchive@@YIHPBDH@Z at 0x48d210 still fails: 140 unmasked mismatches, 32 relocation bytes masked, 8 trailing VC NOP bytes trimmed, BN size 174 bytes, VC object symbol size 112 bytes.

Reviewed difference is the original function's SEH/EH allocation wrapper and cleanup-state scaffold around operator new / Reset / Init. Isolated recompilation emits the same source-level allocation, init, cleanup, current-archive assignment, and PushBackPayload flow but omits the original fs:[0] frame and EH state locals.
The native mount smoke writes a one-record tail-index archive, mounts it, validates current/list payload identity, record lookup, and payload open-by-name behavior, then unloads the archive.
